name: Lezer
summary: Incremental LR (with opt-in GLR) parser system built for CodeMirror 6, generating JavaScript modules that run directly in the browser without WebAssembly overhead.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Lezer is the parser system built for CodeMirror 6. Like tree-sitter, it is designed for incremental parsing, error tolerance, and providing a syntax tree for editor tooling. Unlike tree-sitter's C/C++ foundation, Lezer generates JavaScript modules that run directly in the browser without WebAssembly overhead.

sections.title: Parser architecture
sections.content: Lezer uses an LR parsing algorithm with opt-in GLR for grammars that need it, highly specialized for JavaScript's execution model. The focus is throughput and predictability inside a JS runtime rather than parser-theoretic generality.

sections.title: Compact non-abstract syntax tree
sections.content: Lezer outputs a compact, non-abstract syntax tree: nodes keep track of their width and structure rather than being full JavaScript objects. This minimizes memory consumption and improves cache locality, both critical when the parser runs inside a browser tab.

sections.title: Error recovery
sections.content: The parser seamlessly recovers from syntax errors, guaranteeing that the editor always has a workable syntax tree for highlighting and code navigation. Like tree-sitter, this is the load-bearing property: an editor cannot afford parse failures on the half-typed program in the buffer.

sections.title: Grammar and incremental-edit example
